message:
fix(tests): correct HTTP client test Host header assertion and increase timeout margins

tests/http_client.rs:
- Fixed Host header assertion to match "Host: 127.0.0.1:" (with port)
  instead of "Host: 127.0.0.1\r\n" since the HTTP client now includes
  the ephemeral port in the Host header for non-standard ports.
- Increased sleep durations (150ms→1000ms) and request timeouts
  (30ms→500ms) in 204-no-content and invalid-content-length tests to
  eliminate flaky failures on slow CI runners where the server hadn't
  finished writing the response before the client timed out.
- Updated .beads/issues.jsonl with current bead status entries.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1k69","title":"TypeScript reference capture pipeline for extension conformance","description":"Build a capture harness that runs extensions against the REAL TypeScript pi-mono runtime and records all interactions. This produces the 'ground truth' reference fixtures that our Rust tests diff against.

Pipeline:
1. Install pi-mono (TypeScript) with all dependencies
2. For each extension + scenario: load extension, execute scenario, record:
   - All hostcall request/response pairs
   - Tool registration payloads
   - Event handler return values
   - UI interactions (notifications, prompts, responses)
   - State mutations (session entries, file writes)
3. Output: structured JSON fixtures per extension per scenario
4. Checksum and version-tag each fixture

This is a Node.js script that runs outside our Rust codebase but produces artifacts consumed by our test harness.

File: tests/ext_conformance/capture/ (script + output fixtures)
Note: Requires Node.js/Bun installed in the CI environment for the capture step.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:13:45.535271827Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T06:43:25.385661566Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T06:43:22.894368418Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1k69","depends_on_id":"bd-2vrw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1k69","depends_on_id":"bd-3bje","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:03Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1k69","depends_on_id":"bd-9dqa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:03Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":2840,"issue_id":"bd-1k69","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Closed.  {"id":"bd-1k8t","title":"Build extension API surface matrix","description":"Analyze all 102 extensions (62 official + 40 community) and produce a machine-readable matrix (JSON) documenting for each extension:
- Registration type: tool, slash_command, event_hook, provider, flag, shortcut
- Hostcalls used: pi.tool(), pi.exec(), pi.http(), pi.session.*, pi.ui.*, pi.events()
- Capabilities required: read, write, exec, http, env
- Events listened to: tool_call, tool_result, turn_start, turn_end, session_start, session_end, input, resources_discover, session_before_fork
- I/O patterns: fs-heavy, network-heavy, ui-centric, cpu-heavy, os-heavy
- Complexity classification: small (<2KB), medium (2-10KB), large (>10KB)
- Node API usage: which Node built-ins are imported (fs, path, child_process, etc.)
- Third-party dependencies: npm packages required
- Runtime tier: legacy-js, multi-file, pkg-with-deps, provider-ext

This matrix drives test prioritization and identifies which hostcall/shim gaps block which extensions.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:12:10.191611495Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T06:38:04.618435290Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T06:38:04.618374968Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1k8t","depends_on_id":"bd-1b67","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:56Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1k8t","depends_on_id":"bd-382l","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:56Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1k8t","depends_on_id":"bd-3kpd","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:56Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":2195,"issue_id":"bd-1k8t","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"COMPLETED: Built two comprehensive API surface matrices: (1) docs/extension-catalog.json - 60 official extensions with checksums, tiers, capabilities, file counts (23 small, 25 medium, 12 large).  {"id":"bd-1kfkq","title":"Sync idle AgentSession extension messages into agent context","description":"Non-interactive AgentSession host actions still do not honor idle pi.sendMessage(..., { triggerTurn: true }) semantics from legacy Pi. Unlike the interactive TUI path, AgentSessionHostActions only persists idle custom messages to Session and lacks a runner callback that can reload session history and immediately start a continuation turn. Legacy behavior in agent-session.ts is: idle + triggerTurn -> await agent.prompt(appMessage); nextTurn remains deferred. Rust likely needs either an AgentSession-managed execute-command/continue surface or a host-action trigger hook that can safely drive run_continue_with_abort without duplicating queued/session state.","notes":"Investigation confirmed normal next-prompt behavior is okay because AgentSession reloads Session history before run_text/run_with_content, but immediate idle triggerTurn semantics remain missing.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-08T04:19:09.286474893Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T00:20:29.416212552Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T00:20:29.416182546Z","close_reason":"Implemented in 34c99fbb with current-main E2E coverage in 8e5a5821; verified by remote unit tests plus thread-reported green E2E and cargo check after the rpc blocker fix","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-2pzp","title":"Message queue: pending-messages UI in interactive mode","description":"# Goal
Add a UI region to interactive mode that shows queued messages (steering/follow-up) while the agent is busy.

# Required Behavior
- While busy, display:
  - number of queued steering messages
  - number of queued follow-up messages
  - preview of queued text (truncate long lines)

# UX Notes
- Keep it compact; must not overwhelm the main conversation viewport.
- Prefer consistent styling (dim for follow-up vs bright for steering, etc.).

# Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"bd-2q00","title":"E2E: Full agent loop — message → provider → tool → response","description":"Create E2E integration tests that exercise the full agent loop (src/agent.rs). Tests: (1) simple_conversation: send a user message, get assistant response, no tools. (2) tool_round_trip: send a message that triggers read tool (e.g. 'Read the file Cargo.toml and tell me the package name'), verify the agent calls the tool and returns a response incorporating the tool result. (3) multi_tool: send a message triggering multiple tool calls in sequence. (4) bash_tool_e2e: verify bash tool execution through the agent loop (e.g. 'Run echo hello').  {"id":"bd-2q2v","title":"Unit: CLI input plumbing (stdin + @file + args)","description":"# Goal
Ensure CLI input plumbing is fully covered without mocks, including piped stdin, @file expansion, and message arg ordering.

# Why / User Impact
Input composition bugs create wrong prompts, missing context, or leaked sessions. These tests guarantee predictable input handling.

# Scope (Granular)
- `prepare_initial_message` + `process_file_arguments` with mixed @file + message args.
- Piped stdin vs explicit message args in print mode (`-p`), including empty stdin.
- Image auto-resize flags and error paths for missing/unsupported files.
- Verify **no session side-effects** when `--no-session` / `-p` is used.
- Confirm CLI rejects incompatible modes (e.g., @file in RPC mode).

# Logging / Artifacts
- Use TestHarness; capture created files + stdout/stderr where applicable.
- Log effective composed message (text + image count) for each case.

# Acceptance Criteria
- Tests in `tests/main_cli_selection.rs` or new `tests/cli_inputs.rs`.
- No mocks/fake providers; offline and deterministic.
- Covers both success and error paths with explicit assertions.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

 {"id":"bd-2q73","title":"E2E: Library integration (markdown/syntax/structured-concurrency)","description":"End-to-end verification of library integration with detailed logging.

## Scenarios

### 1. Markdown Rendering E2E
- Agent response with markdown headers, lists, code blocks
- Verify Console output matches expected styling
- Log: render_start, markdown_parse, style_apply, render_complete

### 2. Syntax Highlighting E2E  
- Agent response with code blocks in multiple languages
- Verify syntax tokens colored correctly
- Log: syntax_detect_language, tokenize_start, tokenize_complete

### 3. Structured Concurrency E2E
- Load extension with async event handlers
- Trigger Ctrl+C during handler execution
- Verify cleanup within budget (5s)
- Verify no orphaned tasks
- Log: region_enter, task_spawn, cancellation_start, task_await, region_exit

## JSONL Logging Format
```json
{"ts": "...", "component": "library", "event": "...", "duration_ms": N, "data": {...}}
```

## Artifact Output
- logs/e2e_library_{timestamp}.jsonl
- Captured terminal output for visual inspection

## Files
- tests/e2e/library_integration.rs
- tests/e2e/fixtures/markdown_sample.md
- tests/e2e/fixtures/code_samples/","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:19:27.336439328Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:07.796224949Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:07.796157864Z","close_reason":"Implemented 30+ E2E tests in tests/e2e_library_integration.rs covering: (1) Markdown rendering via rich_rust PiConsole (9 tests: basic, code blocks, multi-language, empty, nested, unterminated, TS, large doc), (2) Theme+Glamour integration (7 tests: dark/light/solarized configs, glamour rendering, empty input, code blocks), (3) Theme discovery/loading (4 tests: discover from dirs, load from file, invalid JSON, round-trip serialization), (4) ExtensionRegion structured concurrency (7 tests: default/custom budget, manager access, into_inner, idempotent shutdown, drop safety, manager shutdown), (5) PiConsole TUI components (5 tests: panel, table, rule, status messages, usage/model info), (6) Console with theme (1 test). All pass, no clippy warnings.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2q73","depends_on_id":"bd-2bct","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:04Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2q73","depends_on_id":"bd-2vie","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:28:04Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-nom.1","title":"Hostcall ABI: WASM host.call Uses Shared Dispatcher (Parity Refactor)","description":"# Goal
Refactor the WASM component hostcall path (`host.call(name, input_json)` in `src/extensions.rs` `mod wasm_host`) so it uses the shared hostcall dispatcher:

`HostCallPayload (from JSON) -> dispatch_host_call_shared() -> HostResultPayload -> WIT return string`

# Current State (Observed)
- WASM hostcalls are implemented in `src/extensions.rs` under `mod wasm_host`, in `impl host::Host for HostState { async fn call(...) }`.
- The current implementation:
  - parses JSON into `HostCallPayload`
  - validates capability mismatch
  - performs policy + logging + timeout
  - dispatches by method with bespoke logic
- A shared dispatcher already exists (`dispatch_host_call_shared()`), but WASM hostcalls do not yet call it.

# Scope
1. Keep input validation that is specific to the WIT contract:
   - `name` must match `payload.method` (or explicitly document any tolerated mismatch).
   - JSON parse errors are `invalid_request`.

2. Replace the bespoke WASM dispatch path with:
   - Build `HostCallContext`:
     - `runtime = "wasm"`
     - `extension_id` (from registration state)
     - `policy`, `tools`, `http`, `fs`, `env_allowlist`, `manager`
   - Call `dispatch_host_call_shared()`.

3. Convert `HostResultPayload` to the WIT return type:
   - On success: return `Ok(<serialized output JSON>)`.
   - On error: return `Err(<serialized HostCallError JSON>)`.
   - Ensure error codes are taxonomy-only (shared dispatcher guarantees this).

4. Ensure logging is not duplicated:
   - `dispatch_host_call_shared()` already emits `host_call.start/end` and `policy.decision`.
   - Remove duplicate logging from the WASM entrypoint so we don’t double-count or drift.

# Tests
- `--features wasm-host` unit/integration tests:
  - capability mismatch -> `invalid_request`
  - denied -> `denied`
  - timeout -> `timeout` with retryable
  - session/ui/events with manager absent -> deterministic taxonomy
  - params_hash parity vs JS for canonical calls (tie into `bd-1uy.1.4`)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No duplicated policy/timeout/logging logic remains in the WASM hostcall entrypoint.
- [ ] WASM hostcall errors and success outputs match the shared dispatcher’s semantics.
- [ ] `cargo test --all-targets --features wasm-host` is green.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrightHeron","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:57:36.997165900Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T11:21:28.916105960Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T11:21:28.916075122Z","close_reason":"Completed: WASM host.call uses shared dispatcher; wasm-host gates green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","hostcall","wasm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-nom.1","depends_on_id":"bd-1uy.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:57Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-nom.1","depends_on_id":"bd-nom","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:57Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-np43","title":"Task: Integrate extension commands into interactive processor","description":"# Task: Integrate Extension Commands into Interactive Processor

## Objective

Modify the interactive command processor to recognize and route extension-registered slash commands.

## Background

The interactive module processes user input, including slash commands. Extension commands must be integrated:
1. When user types /foo, check if foo is an extension command
2. If so, route to extension handler instead of built-in processing
3. Handle the result and display output

## Implementation

### Add Extension Command Check

```rust
async fn process_command(&mut self, input: &str) -> Result<CommandAction> {
    let (cmd, args) = parse_command(input);
    
    // Check built-in commands first (they take precedence)
    match cmd {
        "help" => return self.show_help(),
        "clear" => return self.clear_history(),
        "model" => return self.change_model(args),
        _ => {}
    }
    
    // Check extension commands
    if let Some(runtime) = &self.extension_runtime {
        let ext_commands = runtime.get_registered_commands().await?;
        
        let is_ext_cmd = ext_commands.iter().any(|c| {
            c.name == cmd || c.aliases.contains(&cmd.to_string())
        });
        
        if is_ext_cmd {
            return self.execute_extension_command(cmd, args).await;
        }
    }
    
    Err(anyhow!("Unknown command: /{}", cmd))
}
```

### Execute Extension Command

```rust
async fn execute_extension_command(&mut self, name: &str, args: &str) -> Result<CommandAction> {
    let runtime = self.extension_runtime.as_ref()
        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("No extension runtime"))?;
    
    let result = runtime.execute_command(name, args).await?;
    
    // Display output if any
    if let Some(output) = result.output {
        self.display_output(&output)?;
    }
    
    // Append messages if any
    if let Some(messages) = result.messages {
        for msg in messages {
            self.conversation.push(msg);
        }
    }
    
    if result.continue_loop { Ok(CommandAction::Continue) } else { Ok(CommandAction::Exit) }
}
```

### Update Help Command

```rust
async fn show_help(&self) -> Result<CommandAction> {
    println!("Built-in commands:");
    println!("  /help    - Show this help");
    println!("  /clear   - Clear conversation");
    
    if let Some(runtime) = &self.extension_runtime {
        let ext_commands = runtime.get_registered_commands().await?;
        
        if !ext_commands.is_empty() {
            println!("\nExtension commands:");
            for cmd in ext_commands {
                let usage = cmd.usage.as_deref().unwrap_or("");
                println!("  /{:<8} - {} {}", cmd.name, cmd.description, usage);
            }
        }
    }
    
    Ok(CommandAction::Continue)
}
```

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (7 cases)
1. test_builtin_commands_still_work
2. test_extension_command_recognized
3. test_extension_command_executed_via_js
4. test_output_displayed_correctly
5. test_unknown_command_returns_error
6. test_builtin_commands_take_precedence
7. test_extension_aliases_work

### E2E Test Script (tests/e2e/interactive_commands.sh)

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

LOG_FILE="artifacts/interactive_commands_e2e.jsonl"
mkdir -p artifacts

log() {
    echo "{\"timestamp\":\"$(date -Iseconds)\",\"event\":\"$1\",\"data\":$2}" >> "$LOG_FILE"
}

log "test_start" '{"suite":"interactive_commands"}'

# Create test extension
mkdir -p /tmp/test_interactive_ext
cat > /tmp/test_interactive_ext/index.js << 'EOF'
export function activate(ctx) {
    ctx.registerCommand({
        name: "echo",
        description: "Echo arguments back",
        aliases: ["e"],
        handler: async (args) => ({ output: args || "(empty)" }),
    });
}
EOF

# Test with tmux for interactive mode
if command -v tmux &> /dev/null; then
    SESSION="pi_test_$$"
    tmux new-session -d -s "$SESSION" "pi --extension /tmp/test_interactive_ext"
    sleep 2
    
    # Type /help and capture
    tmux send-keys -t "$SESSION" "/help" Enter
    sleep 1
    HELP_OUTPUT=$(tmux capture-pane -t "$SESSION" -p)
    log "help_output" "{\"contains_echo\":$(echo "$HELP_OUTPUT" | grep -q "echo" && echo true || echo false)}"
    
    # Type /echo test
    tmux send-keys -t "$SESSION" "/echo hello world" Enter
    sleep 1
    ECHO_OUTPUT=$(tmux capture-pane -t "$SESSION" -p)
    log "echo_output" "{\"output\":\"$ECHO_OUTPUT\"}"
    
    # Type /e (alias)
    tmux send-keys -t "$SESSION" "/e alias test" Enter
    sleep 1
    
    # Cleanup
    tmux send-keys -t "$SESSION" "/quit" Enter
    sleep 1
    tmux kill-session -t "$SESSION" 2>/dev/null || true
    
    log "test_end" '{"status":"pass"}'
else
    log "test_skip" '{"reason":"tmux not available"}'
fi

echo "Interactive commands E2E test complete. Logs: $LOG_FILE"
```

- Depends on: bd-ie1g (execute_command)
- Depends on: bd-2qrn (get_registered_commands)

 {"id":"bd-ofcj","title":"Expand proven Pi extension coverage (research→conformance)","description":"# Goal
Dramatically expand the set of Pi extensions that are **proven to work unmodified** across all major extension shapes (skills, prompts, tools, MCP servers, providers, templates, packages).

# Outcome
A curated, version‑pinned catalog with conformance + performance gates that prove compatibility, reliability, and speed.

# Scope
Research → selection → acquisition → conformance → performance → documentation → refresh workflow.

# Non‑Goals
Do not modify third‑party extension source code; we only vendor exact upstream artifacts and validate them.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"WindyCanyon","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:18:34.843067957Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T06:37:44.619954906Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T06:37:44.219563294Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2499,"issue_id":"bd-ofcj","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background: The Pi extension ecosystem is spread across npm, GitHub, and template/skill packs, and current conformance coverage only proves a limited subset.

Considerations: Artifacts must remain byte-for-byte upstream, version-pinned, and validated with correctness + performance + reliability gates.

Exit criteria: A curated catalog and test reports exist, all selected extensions pass or are explicitly excluded with rationale.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:44:52Z"},{"id":2500,"issue_id":"bd-ofcj","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"SCALE TARGET (USER REQUIREMENT)

Evidence requirements
- Deterministic conformance + perf/reliability gates with JSONL logs + diff artifacts.
- Offline/fixture mode in CI; live refresh is opt-in.

 {"id":"bd-rdqz3","title":"Ensure interactive UiShutdown cannot be dropped on full event channel","description":"Fresh-eyes review of bc645f03 found that run_interactive() now tries to unblock the async UI bridge by enqueueing PiMsg::UiShutdown with try_send() on a bounded mpsc channel. Use a guaranteed send path and add focused regression coverage.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-11T05:56:18.780775091Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-11T23:11:03.029637863Z","closed_at":"2026-03-11T23:11:03.029613208Z","close_reason":"Already satisfied on main via 1d995dae: the interactive bridge now uses guaranteed send helpers for UiShutdown and extension UI requests under backpressure, the lossy try_send paths are gone, and focused regressions for saturated event queues remain present in src/interactive/tests.rs and src/interactive/keybindings.rs.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-rhl","title":"Remove tokio and reqwest dependencies","description":"# Remove tokio and reqwest dependencies

## Goal
Complete the HTTP migration by removing tokio and reqwest from Cargo.toml.

## Background
After all providers migrate to asupersync, these dependencies become unused:
- tokio (async runtime)
- tokio-stream (stream utilities)
- reqwest (HTTP client)

This reduces binary size and simplifies the dependency tree.

## Implementation Steps

1. **Update Cargo.toml**
```toml
# Remove these lines:
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
tokio-stream = "0.1"
reqwest = { version = "0.13", ... }

# Ensure asupersync is enabled (currently commented out):
asupersync = { path = "../asupersync", features = ["tls", "tls-native-roots", "sqlite"] }
```

2. **Update main.rs**
```rust
// Before
#[tokio::main]
async fn main() { ... }

// After - use asupersync runtime
fn main() {
    asupersync::runtime::Runtime::new()
        .block_on(async_main())
}
```

3. **Update all async code**
- Replace tokio::spawn with asupersync::spawn
- Replace tokio::time::sleep with asupersync equivalents
- Replace tokio::select! with asupersync patterns
- Update channel usage (if tokio channels used)

4. **Update test harness**
```rust
// Before
#[tokio::test]
async fn test_foo() { ... }

// After
#[asupersync::test]
async fn test_foo() { ... }
```

## Files Affected
- Cargo.toml
- src/main.rs
- src/agent.rs (spawn, select)
- src/tools.rs (bash timeout)
- All test files

## Dependencies
- bd-37l (Anthropic migrated)
- bd-1vo (OpenAI migrated)
- bd-1iy (Gemini migrated)
- bd-ivj (Azure migrated)

## Testing
- cargo build must succeed without tokio
- All tests must pass
- Binary size should decrease (~500KB-1MB)

## Risks
- Some tokio-specific features may need alternatives
- Test harness changes may be extensive

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tokio not in Cargo.toml
- [ ] reqwest not in Cargo.toml
- [ ] cargo build succeeds
- [ ] All tests pass
- [ ] Binary size reduced","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)

 {"id":"bd-rhyl","title":"Define scoring rubric + weights","description":"# Goal
Create a transparent scoring model to rank extensions fairly and consistently.

# Deliverables
- Weighted criteria: popularity (downloads/stars), recency, maintenance activity, license permissiveness, runtime compatibility, reliability risk.
- Clear normalization rules and tie‑breakers.
- Documented rationale for each weight.

# Notes

 {"id":"bd-s1hx","title":"Task: Implement input event (transforming, blocking)","description":"# Task: Implement Input Event (Transforming, Blocking)

## Objective

Dispatch the input event before processing user messages, allowing extensions to transform or block input.

## TypeScript Reference

```typescript
if (runner.hasHandlers('input')) {
    const inputResult = await runner.emit({
        type: 'input',
        content: userMessage.content,
        attachments: userMessage.attachments,
    });
    
    if (inputResult?.block) {
        console.log(inputResult.reason || 'Input blocked');
        return;
    }
    
    if (inputResult?.content) {
        userMessage.content = inputResult.content;
    }
}
```

## Event Details

### input Event
- **When**: Before processing user message
- **Purpose**: Transform, filter, or block input
- **Data**: content, attachments

### Return Value
```rust
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, Deserialize)]
pub struct InputEventResult {
    pub content: Option<String>,      // Transformed content
    pub block: bool,                   // If true, block processing
    pub reason: Option<String>,        // Reason for blocking
}
```

## Implementation

```rust
impl Agent {
    async fn process_user_input(
        &mut self,
        content: String,
        attachments: Vec<Attachment>,
    ) -> Result<Option<UserMessage>> {
        if let Some(dispatcher) = &self.event_dispatcher {
            if dispatcher.has_handlers("input") {
                let event = ExtensionEvent::Input {
                    content: content.clone(),
                    attachments: attachments.clone(),
                };
                
                if let Some(result) = dispatcher.dispatch::<InputEventResult>(event).await? {
                    if result.block {
                        let reason = result.reason.unwrap_or_else(|| "Input blocked".to_string());
                        self.display_message(&reason)?;
                        return Ok(None);
                    }
                    
                    let final_content = result.content.unwrap_or(content);
                    return Ok(Some(UserMessage { content: final_content, attachments }));
                }
            }
        }
        
        Ok(Some(UserMessage { content, attachments }))
    }
}
```

## Use Cases

1. **Macro Expansion**: Replace !date with current date
2. **Content Filter**: Block PII
3. **Translation**: Auto-translate input
4. **Logging**: Record all user inputs

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (5 cases)
1. test_no_handlers_uses_original
2. test_handler_returns_null_uses_original
3. test_handler_transforms_content
4. test_handler_blocks_input
5. test_block_reason_displayed

### E2E Test Script
Extension with macro expansion and content filter, JSONL logging.

## Dependencies

- Depends on: bd-jt3k (Wire Hostcall Dispatcher)
- Part of: bd-10vp (Extension Event Hook Dispatch)

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] input event dispatched before processing
- [ ] Transformation applied correctly
- [ ] Blocking works with reason display
- [ ] 5 unit tests pass
- [ ] E2E test passes","notes":"Progress 2026-02-05: Implemented input event transform/block handling in interactive + AgentSession (apply_input_event_response helper, UpdateLastUserMessage for UI), removed duplicate input dispatch, and ensured before_agent_start dispatch still runs. Fixed clippy/test warnings in tests/provider_factory.rs, tests/agent_loop_vcr.rs, tests/e2e_cli.rs, tests/compaction.rs.  {"id":"bd-sas4","title":"Event dispatch latency benchmarks (p99 less than 5ms)","description":"# Event dispatch latency benchmarks (p99 < 5ms)

## What
Fire 1000+ events of each type through both runtimes, measure handler response time.

## Events to Benchmark
All ExtensionEvent types: tool_call, tool_result, turn_start, turn_end, before_agent_start, input, context, resources_discover, user_bash, session_before_compact, session_before_tree.

## Method
1. Load a test extension that subscribes to all event types
2. Fire 1000 events per type with varied payloads
3. Measure time from dispatch to handler return
4. Compute P50, P95, P99 for each event type in each runtime

## Target
- P99 < 5ms for all event types in Rust
- Rust P50 < TypeScript P50 (should be faster due to native code)

## Output
- Latency report with percentile breakdown per event type","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:25:21.013754536Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T19:26:26.131409126Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T19:26:26.131325280Z","close_reason":"Implemented TS+Rust event dispatch latency benchmark harness and ignored report generator.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-sas4","depends_on_id":"bd-139x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:57Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-sas4","depends_on_id":"bd-150s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-07T03:27:57Z","created_by":"import"}]}
